description:
You are a log analyst who just received an old web-session log that was archived years ago in the ISO-8859-1 character set.  
    Your goal is to bring it into the modern UTF-8 world and then do a quick error-line count for a follow-up report.

    Initial state of the machine:
    1. Directory /home/user/logs already exists.
    2. Inside that directory there is one single file named session_iso8859.log which is encoded in ISO-8859-1.  
       The file contains **exactly five lines** (each terminated with a single LF character) in the following order:

       2023-05-01 12:00:00 INFO  User São logged in
       2023-05-01 12:02:13 ERROR Failed to load Balcón image
       2023-05-01 12:03:45 WARN  Disk almost full
       2023-05-01 12:05:10 ERROR Señor account locked
       2023-05-01 12:07:55 ERROR Timeout on payment gateway

       Note the accented characters (São, Balcón, Señor).  
       These characters are stored correctly according to ISO-8859-1 and must survive the conversion to UTF-8 unchanged.

    Task requirements:
    A. Convert the file /home/user/logs/session_iso8859.log from ISO-8859-1 to UTF-8.  
       • The converted file must be saved as /home/user/logs/session_utf8.log.  
       • All five lines and their order must be preserved.  
       • The destination file must be encoded in UTF-8 (the automated test will verify this with the “file” utility).

    B. Produce a tiny human-readable count of how many lines in the converted file contain the literal word “ERROR”.  
       • Create a directory /home/user/analysis if it does not yet exist.  
       • Write a one-line log file /home/user/analysis/error_summary.log with **exactly** this text (followed by a single LF):  
         ERROR_LINES=3

    C. Nothing else should be created or modified outside the two paths /home/user/logs and /home/user/analysis.

    Verification details the automated grader will check:
    1. /home/user/logs/session_utf8.log exists, is UTF-8 encoded, and its byte sequence represents the five lines shown above.  
    2. /home/user/analysis/error_summary.log exists and contains exactly: “ERROR_LINES=3\n”.

    Use whatever single-command pipeline, redirection, or utility you prefer, but respect the exact file names, paths, and output format so the automated tests pass.
truth:
Pre-existing objects before the agent starts:
    - /home/user/logs/session_iso8859.log (ISO-8859-1), content (five lines, LF line endings):
      2023-05-01 12:00:00 INFO  User São logged in
      2023-05-01 12:02:13 ERROR Failed to load Balcón image
      2023-05-01 12:03:45 WARN  Disk almost full
      2023-05-01 12:05:10 ERROR Señor account locked
      2023-05-01 12:07:55 ERROR Timeout on payment gateway

    Objects that must exist after successful completion:
    1. /home/user/logs/session_utf8.log  
       • UTF-8 encoded file.  
       • Byte-for-byte text equivalent of the above five lines, now in UTF-8.  
    2. /home/user/analysis/error_summary.log  
       • Plain-text UTF-8 file containing exactly “ERROR_LINES=3\n”.

    The grader will run:
      file -b --mime-encoding /home/user/logs/session_utf8.log   # expecting “utf-8”
      cmp -s <expected_UTF8_blob> /home/user/logs/session_utf8.log
      cat /home/user/analysis/error_summary.log | tr -d '\n'     # expecting “ERROR_LINES=3”
name: task_000000_a56d6c48
